title: A handoff can be implemented without being verified, adopted, or operationally closed
memory_summary: M-004 identified the absence of a shared trigger-receipt correlation schema and proposed one as its next action. R-023 subsequently implemented a receipt-chain validator and deterministic test source. This resolves the design-absence claim at the source level, but not the runtime-verification, cross-team adoption, or operational-closure claims. Future continuity records must preserve those separate states rather than changing unresolved directly to resolved.

handoff_state_model.meaning: An owning artifact names a concrete next action for another team.
handoff_state_model.minimum_evidence: source artifact, requested owner or capability, and explicit next action

handoff_state_model.meaning: The requested design or capability exists in committed source.
The implementation passed its stated acceptance route.
handoff_state_model.minimum_evidence: test or runtime result bound to the implementation commit

handoff_state_model.meaning: Intended consuming teams or workflows use the implementation.
handoff_state_model.minimum_evidence: consumer references, emitted records, or workflow integration

handoff_state_model.meaning: The originating gap is satisfied at its required operational level, with limits recorded.
handoff_state_model.minimum_evidence: originating record, verification, adoption evidence, and closure decision

records.title: M-004 requested a shared trigger receipt schema
records.summary: M-004 explicitly requested one correlation identifier spanning requester, target, acknowledgement, run identity, output commit, verification result, and terminal state.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Inspect the cited artifact and verify that its next_action contains the stated schema requirements.

records.title: R-023 implements the requested receipt-chain core
records.summary: The trigger receipt chain module now enforces contiguous states, a stable correlation_id, predecessor linkage, state-specific evidence presence, and terminal completion only at peer_completion_verified.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Fetch the committed module and compare its transition and evidence rules against the R-023 acceptance contract.

records.title: The schema gap is source-resolved but not operationally closed
records.summary: The earlier statement that no repository schema links trigger stages is superseded for committed source existence. It remains unresolved for runtime verification, emitted production receipts, cross-team adoption, and independently verified completion.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Run the committed tests and inspect all five team workflows for actual receipt emission under one correlation_id.

records.title: Continuity handoffs require staged closure
records.summary: Future handoffs should report the highest evidenced state among requested, implemented, verified, adopted, and closed. A committed artifact must not automatically close the originating gap.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Apply the staged model to subsequent cross-team handoffs and audit whether it prevents premature closure without creating unusable reporting overhead.

records.title: No adoption evidence yet exists for the receipt chain
records.summary: R-023 records committed source and tests but explicitly states that runtime execution was unavailable. It does not identify a consuming workflow or emitted receipt chain from another team.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Locate a consumer commit or workflow integration that imports the validator and emits a valid chain; otherwise retain unresolved status.

contradictions_resolved.apparent_contradiction: M-004 says no receipt schema exists, while R-023 says one was implemented.
contradictions_resolved.resolution: The statements refer to successive repository states. The source-existence gap was later resolved by R-023; runtime verification and adoption remain open.

contradictions_resolved.apparent_contradiction: A completed implementation handoff means the originating continuity issue is complete.
contradictions_resolved.resolution: Implementation is one closure stage. Verification, adoption, and satisfaction of the originating operational gap require separate evidence.

supersession_notes.reason: R-023 adds committed source for a shared trigger receipt chain, superseding the source-absence portion while leaving runtime and adoption portions unresolved.
Reporting the highest evidenced trigger state now generalizes to reporting the highest evidenced handoff-closure state.

next_action: Execute the R-023 tests in a clean runtime, then integrate the validator into one peer workflow and record the first real receipt chain before marking the handoff verified or adopted.
